=================================== FAILURES =================================== ____________ test_format_plan_next_steps_plain_hierarchical_format _____________
def test_format_plan_next_steps_plain_hierarchical_format() -> None: """format_plan_next_steps_plain produces hierarchical output.""" output = format_plan_next_steps_plain(42, url="https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42") assert "Implement plan #42:" in output assert "In current wt:" in output assert "In new wt:" in output assert "(dangerously):" in output assert "Checkout plan #42:" in output > assert "Dispatch to queue:" in output E assert 'Dispatch to queue:' in 'Implement plan #42:
 In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 42 --script)" && erk implement
 (dangerou...new-slot --for-plan 42

Dispatch plan #42:
 CLI command: erk pr dispatch 42
 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch'

session.assistant:
## Test Results Summary
**Command 1: `pytest tests/unit/shared/test_next_steps.py -v`**
pytest passed: 15 passed in 0.04s
**Command 2: `pytest packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/output/test_next_steps.py -v`**
pytest failed: 1 failure, 13 passed in 0.05s
**Failure Details:** - Test: `test_format_plan_next_steps_plain_hierarchical_format` - Location: `packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/output/test_next_steps.py:78` - Issue: Expected text "Dispatch to queue:" not found in output - Actual output contains: "Dispatch plan #42:" (without "to queue:") - The test assertion expects `assert "Dispatch to queue:" in output` but the implementation outputs `"Dispatch plan #42:"` instead
**Command 3: `ty check packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/output/next_steps.py`**
ty passed: All checks passed!
---
**Summary:** The type checker passes, but there's a mismatch between the test expectation and implementation output. The test expects the header "Dispatch to queue:" but the implementation is generating "Dispatch plan #42:" instead.
